linting.notes: Project uses pre-commit hooks to run both golangci-lint and prettier. The Makefile 'fmt' target runs './scripts/fmt.sh' which executes 'pre-commit run --all-files'. Golangci-lint enables: errcheck, gosimple, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, typecheck, unused, goconst, gofmt, goimports.

testing.run_command: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(setup-envtest use 1.28 -p path)" KUBEBUILDER_CONTROLPLANE_STOP_TIMEOUT=120s go test -coverprofile cover.out $(go list ./... | grep -v fvt)
testing.mock_patterns: Uses kubebuilder envtest to create fake Kubernetes API server for controller tests
testing.coverage_command: go test -coverprofile cover.out $(go list ./... | grep -v fvt)
testing.notes: Unit tests use kubebuilder's envtest framework which requires etcd, kube-apiserver binaries provided by setup-envtest. FVT (functional verification tests) in fvt/ directory require an actual Kubernetes cluster with ModelMesh deployed and are run separately via 'make fvt'. The test command excludes fvt package from unit tests.

ci.notes: CI uses a custom development container (kserve/modelmesh-controller-develop) built from Dockerfile.develop which includes all build tools, kubebuilder, pre-commit, etc. The lint and test workflows build this dev container first, then run make fmt and make test inside it. For local validation without the dev container, install pre-commit, setup-envtest, and run commands directly.

build.notes: The Makefile provides 'make build.develop' to build the dev container and 'make build' to build the runtime container. The 'make manager' target builds the binary locally. Development workflow typically uses the containerized environment via './scripts/develop.sh' which mounts the local repo into the dev container.

conventions.test_naming: Go convention: TestXxx functions or Ginkgo Describe/It blocks
conventions.import_style: Grouped imports with standard library first, then third-party, then local packages
conventions.notes: Test files follow standard Go naming. Ginkgo is used for FVT tests and some controller tests. Pre-commit enforces gofmt and goimports for consistent formatting.

setup_commands: apt-get update && apt-get install -y make git curl python3 python3-pip nodejs npm

lint_commands.notes: Exit code 1 with lint violations found (4 issues). Command works correctly - golangci-lint and prettier both ran.

test_commands.command: KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS="$(setup-envtest use 1.28 -p path)" KUBEBUILDER_CONTROLPLANE_STOP_TIMEOUT=120s go test -coverprofile cover.out $(go list ./... | grep -v fvt)
test_commands.notes: All unit tests passed. 12 packages tested with coverage ranging from 10.5% to 54.3%.

container_recipe.notes: The base golang:1.25 image is Debian-based. Python3 and Node.js are required for pre-commit hooks (golangci-lint is installed via pre-commit, prettier needs Node). The setup-envtest tool downloads kubebuilder test binaries (etcd, kube-apiserver) needed for controller tests. FVT tests cannot run in this container - they require a real Kubernetes cluster.
